The fire prompted France to deploy two firefighting Canadair planes from the country's south as crews battled to contain the blaze, the first time such aircraft have been used in the greater Paris region.

items.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit

items.title: World No. 1 defends Wimbledon title
items.description: Jannik Sinner is Wimbledon champion once again! The world No. 1 defeated Alexander Zverev 6-7, 7-6, 6-3, 6-4 in a thrilling four-set final to defend his title. The turning point came when Zverev slipped and appeared to injure his knee in the third set, allowing Sinner to seize control. The Italian kept his composure, sealed his fifth Grand Slam crown, and celebrated by collapsing onto Centre Court in delight. For Zverev, there was still some consolation as he'll rise to world No. 2 despite the defeat. Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Italian tennis journalist, Ubaldo Scanagatta.

items.description: Mali's army has regained control of the key northern town of Anefis after fighting against rebel militants for nearly a week, Tuareg separatists and a military source said Friday.

items.title: Ghana extradites influencer "Abu Trica" to US over alleged $8m romance fraud scheme
items.description: A well-known Ghanaian social media figure, Frederick Kumi, better known online as Abu Trica, has been sent to the United States to stand trial over accusations that he ran a large-scale romance scam targeting older Americans.

items.title: U.S. to seek extradition of Bishnoi, Nagra: official
items.description: The U.S. has also charged Bishnoi’s aide Goldy Brar with ordering Nijjar’s assassination; the FBI has announced a $50,000 reward for information leading to Brar’s arrest

items.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
items.description: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

items.title: India, New Zealand elevate ties to strategic partnership; target ₹35,000 crore trade by 2030
items.description: A joint statement noted that Narendra Modi and Christopher Luxon exchanged views on the Indo-Pacific, reaffirming their shared commitment to a free, open, and prosperous region

items.title: Lawyers say Ebola patient placed in Equatorial Guinea hotel housing US deportees
items.description: Lawyers and two of the deportees say a man suspected of having the virus was brought to the hotel last week by people in hazmat suits.

items.title: Efforts on to fly home remains of three Andhra tourists killed in Vietnam boat accident
items.description: Embassy of India in Vietnam has finalised the authorised agency that will facilitate the repatriation of the mortal remains of the deceased to India

items.title: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
items.description: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

items.title: 'We have to stay grounded': France prepare for Spain showdown
items.description: France are preparing for a highly anticipated semi-final against Span following their qualification thanks to a win against Morocco. The Spanish team is expected to be France's toughest challenge yet, as La Roja have only let in one goal so far. FRANCE 24's James Vasina tells us more.

items.title: Germany: Iraqi sentenced to life for enslaving Yazidi girls
items.description: The man and his former partner are accused of holding two Yazidi girls captive for years while subjecting them to forced labor, abuse and torture before handing them over to other IS members.

items.title: WHO warns Sudan's cholera outbreak may worsen amid conflict and rains
items.description: The latest outbreak has claimed more than 100 lives so far and infected more than 1,300 others across several states.

items.title: Muslim judge in India faces death threats after convicting 'cow vigilantes'
items.description: Tabassum Khan has been facing online abuse after convicting 14 Hindu men in a lynching case.

items.title: Iran war: US carries out fresh strikes near Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The US army said the strikes near the vital waterway were intended to "degrade" Tehran's ability to attack commercial ships. Iran has retaliated by targeting US allies in the region. Follow DW for the latest.

items.title: Iran attacks US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan
items.description: Iran launches attacks on Gulf states, targeting US military assets in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan.

items.title: England, Argentina to renew bitter rivalry in World Cup semi-final
items.description: The Albiceleste and their talisman Lionel Messi must defeat England in Atlanta to keep alive their hopes of becoming the first team to win back-to-back World Cups since Brazil in 1962. Leer más

items.title: Migrant backlash risks South Africa’s economy
items.description: Economists warn that if thousands of foreign workers leave, it could damage the very businesses and jobs protesters say they want to protect.

items.title: Nippon Paint offers $8.6 billion for AkzoNobel decorative paints unit
FIFA World Cup: Are Messi, Mbappe, Yamal, Kane in the best semifinals yet?
items.description: France, Spain, England and Argentina have the stars for the semifinals, but will the performances match the reputations?

items.title: When London stank to high heaven
items.description: In 1858, London sweltered through an unbearable heat wave. The River Thames reeked so badly it left people gasping for air. "The Great Stink" went down in history — and ultimately gave London a modern sewer system.

items.title: US and Iran trade strikes as ceasefire comes under growing strain
items.description: Iran's IRGC says it attacked sites linked to the US military in Kuwait, Bahrain and Oman after latest US bombardment.

items.title: Thai zoo celebrates famous pygmy hippo's second birthday
items.description: In her enclosure at Khao Kheow Open Zoo in Chon Buri, a province east of Bangkok in Thailand, the 2-year-old Moo Deng approached the cake where her mother Jona had already begun digging in

items.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
items.description: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

items.title: Kimmit: Renewed US-Iran fighting could reignite wider regional conflict
items.description: Retired US General Mark Kimmitt warns renewed attacks in the Strait of Hormuz could push the US and Iran

items.title: INEC Portal: APC mocks opposition over deadline extension for uploading candidates’ names
items.description: The APC says that though INEC acted within its statutory powers and administrative discretion, the circumstances that led to the extension exposed the organisational shortcomings of the opposition parties and their capacity to govern. The post INEC Portal: APC mocks opposition over deadline extension for uploading candidates’ names appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
items.description: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Heavy floods submerge roads, vehicles in northern China
items.description: Authorities warned the storm would dump torrential rain across the Provinces of Jilin, Liaoning, Hebei, Shandong, Jiangsu and Anhui, ‌exacerbating flood risks in areas that had already been soaked by earlier ​downpours

items.title: Indian tourists among 15 killed as speedboat capsizes in Vietnam
items.description: Local media say 21 people were saved after the boat overturned in rough seas near an island in south Vietnam.

items.title: Vietnam boat tragedy: Bodies of 15 Indian tourists to be sent home soon
items.description: The Embassy added that, along with the Consulate teams and the Vietnamese authorities, it remains committed to facilitating the earliest repatriation

items.title: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire
items.description: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

items.title: Spain: Nine injured in fourth San Fermín bull run in Pamplona
items.description: The fourth San Fermin bull run was once again a fast and dangerous dash along the cobbled streets of Pamplona.

items.title: Chinese geologists question safety of Brahmaputra Mega-Dam in Tibet being built over active seismic fault line
items.description: China formally began construction of the $167.8-billion dam on the Brahmaputra in Tibet last July

items.title: Coroner calls for change after man died while paramedic waited outside
items.description: A paramedic who chose not to force entry to a house, despite being told a man was inside and needed medical attention, had "a flawed understanding" of his powers to enter the property, an inquest finds.

items.title: IMF confirms 3.5% growth forecast for Argentina in 2026
items.description: Multilateral lender’s chief Kristalina Georgieva to visit Buenos Aires this month; International Monetary Fund sees Argentina’s economy expanding 4% in 2027. Leer más

items.title: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
items.description: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.

items.title: How Indian scientists are mapping the brain's last frontier
items.description: India's new brainstem atlas offers scientists an unprecedented map of one of the brain's least known regions.

items.title: Name revealed of Sydney daycare worker facing 329 alleged child abuse charges
items.description: A Sydney childcare worker faces more than 320 charges over his alleged abuse of 136 children over a 16-year period, as police reveal a list of locations where he worked.

items.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
items.description: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

items.title: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
items.description: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

items.title: Syria unveils archaeological treasures returned from France after 15 years
items.description: When French President Emmanuel Macron visited Syria earlier this week, he brought with him a number of archaeological treasures that had been held in France since 2011.

items.title: India's Tata Consultancy Services plans up to 8,900 AI deployment engineers, seeks AI acquisitions
Spain edges Belgium and will face France in World Cup semifinal
items.description: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.

items.title: EU to propose limit on kids' social media access, von der Leyen says
What would it mean for Ukraine if it actually starts producing Patriot missiles?
items.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.

items.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
items.description: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling to make ends meet. One city that is particularly deep in the red is Oberhausen.

items.title: Nine arrests as Argentina fans celebrate dramatic World Cup win over Egypt
items.description: Joyous celebrations at Buenos Aires' Obelisk marred by incidents between fans and police; At least nine arrested, according to local media. Leer más

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Jude Bellingham double sends jubilant England into World Cup semis
items.description: England beat Norway 2-1 in extra time on 12 July 2026 to reach the World Cup semifinals. Fans celebrated in London after a Jude Bellingham double sent the Three Lions through to face Argentina.
